name: Fauna (Serverless Document Database)
pricing_notes: Free: 100K reads, 50K writes/day; Pro from $25/mo; usage-based billing

auth.notes: Secret key per database; role-based access with attribute-based conditions

performance.notes: Globally distributed; multi-region reads fast; write consensus slower

agent_readiness.notes: Globally distributed serverless document database with FQL (Fauna Query Language). from fauna import fql, Client. client = Client(secret='fn...'). result = client.query(fql('Product.all()'). FQL v10: native document querying with type safety. ACID transactions. Temporal queries (time-travel). No schema required. GraphQL API alternative to FQL. Serverless: no cluster management. Database-per-tenant easy. Strong consistency globally. Used in JAMstack apps.

gotchas: FQL v10 breaking changes from FQL v4 — check version carefully
Cold start latency for first queries
Pricing complexity at scale (read unit costs add up)
No local development server (shell or Fauna Dev Docker only)
best_when: Serverless applications needing global distribution without ops
When strong consistency across global regions is required
avoid_when: Teams preferring SQL (FQL has learning curve)
summary: Fauna is a serverless globally-distributed document database with FQL (Fauna Query Language). client.query(fql('Product.all()')) for ACID-compliant document queries. Temporal queries (time-travel). No schema required. Strong consistency globally. Database-per-tenant for multi-tenant SaaS. Free tier available. FQL v10 has breaking changes from v4. Best for JAMstack/serverless apps needing global distribution without database ops.
